name: cytosolic ribosome
description: "A ribosome located in the cytosol." [GOC:mtg_sensu]

name: ribosome
description: "An intracellular organelle, about 200 A in diameter, consisting of RNA and protein. It is the site of protein biosynthesis resulting from translation of messenger RNA (mRNA). It consists of two subunits, one large and one small, each containing only protein and RNA. Both the ribosome and its subunits are characterized by their sedimentation coefficients, expressed in Svedberg units (symbol: S). Hence, the prokaryotic ribosome (70S) comprises a large (50S) subunit and a small (30S) subunit, while the eukaryotic ribosome (80S) comprises a large (60S) subunit and a small (40S) subunit. Two sites on the ribosomal large subunit are involved in translation, namely the aminoacyl site (A site) and peptidyl site (P site). Ribosomes from prokaryotes, eukaryotes, mitochondria, and chloroplasts have characteristically distinct ribosomal proteins." [ISBN:0198506732]

name: cytoplasmic translation
description: "The chemical reactions and pathways resulting in the formation of a protein in the cytoplasm. This is a ribosome-mediated process in which the information in messenger RNA (mRNA) is used to specify the sequence of amino acids in the protein." [GOC:hjd]

name: structural constituent of ribosome
description: "The action of a molecule that contributes to the structural integrity of the ribosome." [GOC:mah]

name: ribosomal subunit
description: "Either of the two subunits of a ribosome: the ribosomal large subunit or the ribosomal small subunit." [GOC:jl]

name: structural molecule activity
description: "The action of a molecule that contributes to the structural integrity of a complex." [GOC:mah, GOC:vw]

name: translation
description: "The cellular metabolic process in which a protein is formed, using the sequence of a mature mRNA or circRNA molecule to specify the sequence of amino acids in a polypeptide chain. Translation is mediated by the ribosome, and begins with the formation of a ternary complex between aminoacylated initiator methionine tRNA, GTP, and initiation factor 2, which subsequently associates with the small subunit of the ribosome and an mRNA or circRNA. Translation ends with the release of a polypeptide chain from the ribosome." [GOC:go_curators]

name: ribonucleoprotein complex
description: "A macromolecular complex that contains both RNA and protein molecules." [GOC:krc, GOC:vesicles]

name: cytosol

name: cytosolic small ribosomal subunit

name: cytosolic large ribosomal subunit

description: "Suppression of the occurrence of translational errors, such as codon-anticodon mis-paring, during the process of translation of a protein using an mRNA template." [GOC:hjd, ISBN:9781936113460, PMID:21841312]

name: Abnormal erythrocyte adenosine deaminase activity
description: Activity of adenosine deaminase in red blood cells outside the limits of normal.

name: Macrocytic dyserythropoietic anemia
description: Macrocytic dyserythropoietic anemia

name: Radial artery aplasia
description: Congenital absence of the radial artery.

name: Elevated red cell adenosine deaminase activity
description: Increase in the level of adenosine deaminase (ADA), an enzyme involved in purine metabolism, within erythrocytes. ADA is involved in the catabolism of adenosine.

name: Abnormal radial artery morphology
description: Any structural anomaly of the radial artery.

name: Pure red cell aplasia
description: A type of anemia resulting from suppression of erythropoiesis with little or no abnormality of leukocyte or platelet production. Erythroblasts are virtually absent in bone marrow; however, leukocyte and platelet production show little or no reduction.

name: Erythroid hypoplasia
description: Decreased count of erythroid precursor cells, that is, erythroid lineage cells in the bone marrow.

name: Reticulocytopenia
description: A reduced number of reticulocytes in the peripheral blood.

name: Sprengel anomaly
description: A congenital skeletal deformity characterized by the elevation of one scapula (thus, one scapula is located superior to the other).

name: Partial duplication of thumb phalanx
description: A partial duplication, depending on severity leading to a broad or bifid appearance, affecting one or more of the phalanges of the thumb. As opposed to a complete duplication there is still a variable degree of fusion between the duplicated bones.

name: Partial duplication of the phalanx of hand
description: A partial duplication, depending on severity leading to a broad or bifid appearance, affecting one or more of the phalanges of the hand. As opposed to a complete duplication there is still a variable degree of fusion between the duplicated bones.

name: Persistence of hemoglobin F
description: Hemoglobin F (HbF) contains two globin alpha chains and two globin gamma chains. It is the main form of hemoglobin in the fetus during the last seven months of intrauterine development and in the half year of postnatal life. In adults it normally makes up less than one percent of all hemoglobin. This term refers to an increase in HbF above this limit. In beta thalassemia major, it may represent over 90 percent of all hemoglobin, and in beta thalassemia minor it may make up between 0.5 to 4 percent.

name: Adenocarcinoma of the colon
description: Adenocarcinoma of the colon

name: Osteosarcoma
description: A malignant bone tumor that usually develops during adolescence and usually affects the long bones including the tibia, femur, and humerus. The typical symptoms of osteosarcoma comprise bone pain, fracture, limitation of motion, and tenderness or swelling at the site of the tumor.

name: Adenocarcinoma of the large intestine
description: A malignant epithelial tumor with a glandular organization that originates in the large intestine.

name: 90S preribosome
description: "A large ribonucleoprotein complex considered to be the earliest preribosomal complex. In S. cerevisiae, it has a size of 90S and consists of the 35S pre-rRNA, early-associating ribosomal proteins most of which are part of the small ribosomal subunit, the U3 snoRNA and associated proteins." [GOC:krc, GOC:vw, PMID:12150911, PMID:12957375, PMID:15120992]

name: Normochromic anemia
description: Normochromic anemia

name: Malignant genitourinary tract tumor
description: The presence of a malignant neoplasm of the genital system.

name: Adenocarcinoma of the intestines
description: A malignant epithelial tumor with a glandular organization that originates in the intestines.

name: Duplication of thumb phalanx
description: Complete or partial duplication of the phalanges of the thumb. Depending on the severity, the appearance on x-ray can vary from a notched phalanx (the duplicated bone is almost completely fused with the phalanx), a partially fused appearance of the two bones (bifid), two separate bones appearing side to side, or completely duplicated phalanges (proximal and distal phalanx of the thumb and/or 1st metacarpal). In contrast to the phalanges of the digits 2-5 (proximal, middle and distal), the proximal phalanx of the thumb is embryologically equivalent to the middle phalanges of the other digits, whereas the first metacarpal is embryologically of phalangeal origin and as such equivalent to the proximal phalanges of the other digits.

name: Abnormality of the thenar eminence
description: An abnormality of the thenar eminence, i.e., of the muscle on the palm of the human hand just beneath the thumb.

name: Absent thumb
description: Absent thumb, i.e., the absence of both phalanges of a thumb and the associated soft tissues.

name: Triphalangeal thumb
description: A thumb with three phalanges in a single, proximo-distal axis. Thus, this term applies if the thumb has an accessory phalanx, leading to a digit like appearance of the thumb.

name: Increased mean corpuscular volume
description: Larger than normal size of erythrocytes.

name: protein-RNA complex assembly
description: "The aggregation, arrangement and bonding together of proteins and RNA molecules to form a ribonucleoprotein complex." [GOC:jl]

name: Abnormal mean corpuscular volume
description: A deviation from normal of the mean corpuscular volume, or mean cell volume (MCV) of red blood cells, usually taken to be 80 to 100 femtoliters.

name: protein-RNA complex organization
description: "Any process in which macromolecules aggregate, disaggregate, or are modified, resulting in the formation, disassembly, or alteration of a ribonucleoprotein complex." [GOC:mah]

name: primary metabolic process
description: "The chemical reactions and pathways involving those compounds which are formed as a part of the normal anabolic and catabolic processes. These processes take place in most, if not all, cells of the organism." [GOC:go_curators]

name: Abnormal number of erythroid precursors
description: A deviation from the normal count of erythroid precursor cells, that is, erythroid lineage cells in the bone marrow.

name: Finger aplasia
description: A developmental defect resulting in the presence of fewer than the normal number of fingers (i.e., aplasia of one or more fingers).

name: maturation of SSU-rRNA from tricistronic rRNA transcript (SSU-rRNA, 5.8S rRNA, LSU-rRNA)
description: "Any process involved in the maturation of a precursor Small SubUnit (SSU) ribosomal RNA (rRNA) molecule into a mature SSU-rRNA molecule from the pre-rRNA molecule originally produced as a tricistronic rRNA transcript that contains the Small Subunit (SSU) rRNA, 5.8S rRNA, and the Large Subunit (LSU) in that order from 5' to 3' along the primary transcript." [GOC:curators]

name: Abnormal reticulocyte morphology
description: A reticulocyte abnormality.

name: macromolecule metabolic process
description: "The chemical reactions and pathways involving macromolecules, any molecule of high relative molecular mass, the structure of which essentially comprises the multiple repetition of units derived, actually or conceptually, from molecules of low relative molecular mass." [GOC:mah]

name: Gastrointestinal carcinoma

name: Abnormal hemoglobin

name: Malignant gastrointestinal tract tumors
description: Malignant gastrointestinal tract tumors

name: ribosomal small subunit assembly
description: "The aggregation, arrangement and bonding together of constituent RNAs and proteins to form the small ribosomal subunit." [GOC:jl, PMID:30467428]

name: Cleft soft palate
description: Cleft of the soft palate (also known as the velum, or muscular palate) as a result of a developmental defect occurring between the 7th and 12th week of pregnancy. Cleft soft palate can cause functional abnormalities of the Eustachian tube with resulting middle ear anomalies and hearing difficulties, as well as speech problems associated with hypernasal speech due to velopharyngeal insufficiency.

name: Short thumb
description: Hypoplasia (congenital reduction in size) of the thumb.

name: Macrocytic anemia
description: A type of anemia characterized by increased size of erythrocytes with increased mean corpuscular volume (MCV) and increased mean corpuscular hemoglobin (MCH).

name: Nonimmune hydrops fetalis
description: A type of hydrops fetalis in which there is no identifiable circulating antibody to red blood cell antigens .

description: Factor: HMO1; motif: NNYTCYGCCTRGRSM

name: Horseshoe kidney
description: A connection of the right and left kidney by an isthmus of functioning renal parenchyma or fibrous tissue that crosses the midline.

name: membraneless organelle assembly

name: Aplasia/Hypoplasia of the thumb

name: Acute myeloid leukemia
description: A form of leukemia characterized by overproduction of an early myeloid cell.

name: Abnormal scapula morphology
description: Any abnormality of the scapula, also known as the shoulder blade.

name: Webbed neck
description: Pterygium colli is a congenital skin fold that runs along the sides of the neck down to the shoulders. It involves an ectopic fibrotic facial band superficial to the trapezius muscle. Excess hair-bearing skin is also present and extends down the cervical region well beyond the normal hairline.

name: Abnormality of the musculature of the hand
description: Abnormality of the musculature of the hand

name: Neoplasm of the large intestine

name: Low anterior hairline
description: Distance between the hairline (trichion) and the glabella (the most prominent point on the frontal bone above the root of the nose), in the midline, more than two SD below the mean. Alternatively, an apparently decreased distance between the hairline and the glabella.

name: Developmental glaucoma
description: Glaucoma which forms during the early years of a child's life is called developmental or congenital glaucoma.

name: Hydrops fetalis
description: The abnormal accumulation of fluid in two or more fetal compartments, including ascites, pleural effusion, pericardial effusion, and skin edema.

name: Renal agenesis
description: Agenesis, that is, failure of the kidney to develop during embryogenesis and development.

name: Duplication of bones involving the upper extremities

name: Duplication of phalanx of hand
description: This term applies if one or more of the phalanges of the hand are either partially duplicated, depending on severity leading to a broad or bifid appearance of the phalanges, or completely duplicated.

name: Duplication of hand bones
description: Duplication of hand bones

name: Abnormality of thumb phalanx
description: A structural anomaly of one or more phalanges of the thumb.

name: Sarcoma
description: A connective tissue neoplasm formed by proliferation of mesodermal cells. Bone and soft tissue sarcomas are the main types of sarcoma. Sarcoma is usually highly malignant.

name: metabolic process
description: "A cellular process consisting of the biochemical pathways by which a living organism transforms chemical substances. This includes including anabolism (biosynthetic process) and catabolism (catabolic process). Metabolic processes includes the transformation of small molecules, as well  macromolecular processes such as DNA repair and replication, protein synthesis and degradation." [GOC:go_curators, ISBN:0198547684]

name: RNA processing
description: "Any process involved in the conversion of one or more primary RNA transcripts into one or more mature RNA molecules." [GOC:mah]

name: Coarctation of aorta
description: Coarctation of the aorta is a narrowing or constriction of a segment of the aorta.

name: Abnormal soft palate morphology

name: melatonin binding

name: organelle assembly
description: "The aggregation, arrangement and bonding together of a set of components to form an organelle. An organelle is an organized structure of distinctive morphology and function. Includes the nucleus, mitochondria, plastids, vacuoles, vesicles, ribosomes and the cytoskeleton. Excludes the plasma membrane." [GOC:mah]

name: Pallor
description: Abnormally pale skin.

name: Acute leukemia
description: A clonal (malignant) hematopoietic disorder with an acute onset, affecting the bone marrow and the peripheral blood. The malignant cells show minimal differentiation and are called blasts, either myeloid blasts (myeloblasts) or lymphoid blasts (lymphoblasts).

name: Abnormal localization of kidney
description: An abnormal site of the kidney.

name: Neoplasm of the skeletal system
description: A tumor (abnormal growth of tissue) of the skeleton.

name: Lethargy
description: A state of fatigue, either physical or mental slowness and sluggishness, with difficulties in initiating or performing simple tasks. Distinguished from apathy which implies indifference and a lack of desire or interest in the task. A person with lethargy may have the desire, but not the energy to engage in personal or socially relevant tasks.

name: Myelodysplasia
description: Clonal hematopoietic stem cell disorders characterized by dysplasia (ineffective production) in one or more hematopoietic cell lineages, leading to anemia and cytopenia.

name: Developmental cataract
description: A cataract that occurs congenitally as the result of a developmental defect, in contrast to the majority of cataracts that occur in adulthood as the result of degenerative changes of the lens.

name: ribosomal subunit export from nucleus
description: "The directed movement of a ribosomal subunit from the nucleus into the cytoplasm." [GOC:ai]

name: Short neck
description: Diminished length of the neck.

name: ribosome localization
description: "A process in which a ribosome is transported to, and/or maintained in, a specific location." [GOC:mah]

name: Microtia
description: Underdevelopment of the external ear.

name: Decreased total neutrophil count
description: Abnormal decrease of absolute number of neutrophils in the blood, per microlitre, compared to a reference range for a given sex and age-group.

name: Abnormality of the cervical spine
description: Any abnormality of the cervical vertebral column.

name: Decreased total leukocyte count
description: An abnormal decreased number of leukocytes in the blood.

name: Abnormal total neutrophil count
description: A deviation from the normal range of neutrophil cell counts in the circulation.

name: Neoplasm of the gastrointestinal tract
description: A tumor (abnormal growth of tissue) of the gastrointestinal tract.

name: Cleft lip
description: A gap in the lip or lips.

name: Abnormal granulocyte count
description: Abnormal increase or decrease of absolute number of granulocytes in the blood, per microliter, compared to a reference range for a given sex and age-group.

name: nucleic acid binding
description: "Binding to a nucleic acid." [GOC:jl]

name: Aplasia/Hypoplasia of the external ear
description: The presence of aplasia or developmental hypoplasia of all or part of the external ear.

name: Neoplasm of the genitourinary tract
description: A tumor (abnormal growth of tissue) of the genitourinary system.

name: Aplasia/Hypoplasia of the ear
description: The presence of aplasia or developmental hypoplasia of the ear.

name: regulation of translational fidelity
description: "Any process that modulates the ability of the translational apparatus to interpret the genetic code." [GOC:dph, GOC:tb]

name: Anemia of inadequate production
description: A kind of anemia characterized by inadequate production of erythrocytes.

[GOC:krc, PMID:10690410]

name: Digestive system neoplasm
description: A tumor (abnormal growth of tissue) of the digestive system.

name: Renal hypoplasia/aplasia
description: Absence or underdevelopment of the kidney.

name: Abnormal granulocyte morphology
description: Any structural abnormality or abnormal count of granulocytes.

name: Abnormality of the musculature of the upper limbs
description: Abnormality of the musculature of the upper limbs

name: ribosomal large subunit biogenesis
description: "A cellular process that results in the biosynthesis of constituent macromolecules, assembly, and arrangement of constituent parts of a large ribosomal subunit; includes transport to the sites of protein synthesis." [GOC:jl]

name: nuclear export
description: "The directed movement of substances out of the nucleus." [GOC:ai]

name: Neoplasm by histology
description: Neoplasm categorized according to type of histological abnormality.

name: Abnormality of the frontal hairline
description: An anomaly in the placement or shape of the hairline (trichion) on the forehead, that is, the border between skin on the forehead that has head hair and that does not.

name: Abnormal fetal morphology
description: Any structural anomaly of the fetus.

name: Leukemia
description: A cancer of the blood and bone marrow characterized by an abnormal proliferation of leukocytes.

name: Short finger
description: Abnormally short finger associated with developmental hypoplasia.

name: Abnormal myeloid leukocyte morphology
description: An abnormality of myeloid leukocytes.

name: amide binding
description: "Binding to an amide, any derivative of an oxoacid in which an acidic hydroxy group has been replaced by an amino or substituted amino group." [GOC:mah]

name: Small for gestational age
description: Smaller than normal size according to sex and gestational age related norms, defined as a weight below the 10th percentile for the gestational age.

name: protein-containing complex localization
description: "A localization process that acts on a protein complex; the complex is transported to, or maintained in, a specific location." [GOC:mah]

name: Cleft palate
description: Cleft palate is a developmental defect of the palate resulting from a failure of fusion of the palatine processes and manifesting as a separation of the roof of the mouth (soft and hard palate).

name: Orofacial cleft
description: The presence of a cleft (gap, opening, or groove) in the oral cavity, including cleft of the upper lip and/or cleft of the palate. Cleft of the upper lip is visible as a groove or fissure in the lip, most frequently due to a congenital failure of the maxillary and median nasal processes to fuse. Cleft palate is characterized by a grooved depression or fissure in the roof of the mouth, most often resulting from a congenital failure of the palate to fuse properly. Clefts of the lip and palate can occur individually or together. It is preferable to code each defect separately.

name: Craniofacial cleft
description: Congenital abnormality with cleft (gap or opening) in the craniofacial bones. Craniofacial cleft includes facial cleft, orofacial clef, and cleft of the jaw.

name: Aplasia/Hypoplasia of fingers
description: Small/hypoplastic or absent/aplastic fingers.

name: ribosomal large subunit assembly
description: "The aggregation, arrangement and bonding together of constituent RNAs and proteins to form the large ribosomal subunit." [GOC:jl, PMID:30467428]

name: Abnormal platelet count
description: Abnormal number of platelets per volume of blood. In a healthy adult, a normal platelet count is between 150,000 and 450,000 per microliter of blood.

name: Abnormal platelet morphology
description: An anomaly in platelet form, ultrastructure, or intracellular organelles.

name: Abnormality of the hairline
description: The hairline refers to the outline of hair of the head. An abnormality of the hairline can refer to an unusually low or high border between areas of the scalp with and without hair or to abnormal projections of scalp hair.

name: cellular component organization or biogenesis
description: "A process that results in the biosynthesis of constituent macromolecules, assembly, arrangement of constituent parts, or disassembly of a cellular component." [GOC:mah]

name: Abnormal hair pattern
description: An abnormality of the distribution of hair growth.

name: Ocular anterior segment dysgenesis
description: Abnormal development (dysgenesis) of the anterior segment of the eye globe. These structures are mainly of mesenchymal origin.

name: Abnormal large intestine morphology
description: Any abnormality of the large intestine.

name: cytoplasm
description: "The contents of a cell excluding the plasma membrane and nucleus, but including other subcellular structures." [ISBN:0198547684]

name: Abnormal thumb morphology
description: An abnormal structure of the first digit of the hand.

name: Depressed nasal bridge
description: Posterior positioning of the nasal root in relation to the overall facial profile for age.

name: Abnormality of thrombocytes
description: An abnormality of platelets.

name: translational elongation
description: "The successive addition of amino acid residues to a nascent polypeptide chain during protein biosynthesis." [GOC:ems]

name: Congenital abnormal hair pattern
description: A congenital abnormality of the distribution of hair growth.
